# UX Requirements Checklist: Web UI Graph Browser

**Purpose**: Validate that SPEC-006 UX requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and measurable for the developer-tool browser surface.
**Created**: 2026-07-15
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are repo switcher and selected repository status requirements defined as persistent global shell elements across primary workflows? [Completeness, Spec FR-031]
- [x] CHK002 Are repository switching reset rules specified for symbol, graph, impact, and chat context so cross-repository state cannot remain ambiguous? [Completeness, Spec FR-032]
- [x] CHK003 Are global search requirements documented for selected-repository scope, empty results, ambiguous results, and stale-data results? [Completeness, Spec US2, Spec Edge Cases, Spec FR-005, Spec FR-006, Spec FR-036]
- [x] CHK004 Are symbol detail requirements complete enough to make the detail view the canonical anchor for metadata, source context, relationships, graph, impact, and chat-with-context? [Completeness, Spec FR-008, Spec FR-033]
- [x] CHK005 Are graph canvas requirements defined for pan, zoom, filtering, selection, focus, fit/reset, expansion, truncation, and synchronized selected-node context? [Completeness, Spec FR-012, Spec FR-013, Spec FR-034, Spec FR-035]
- [x] CHK006 Are impact panel requirements documented for affected symbols, affected files, traversal limits, stale inputs, and incomplete result conditions? [Completeness, Spec FR-015, Spec FR-016]
- [x] CHK007 Are chat panel requirements documented for configured, disabled, dormant, misconfigured, pending-bundle, fallback, rate-limited, and error states? [Completeness, Spec FR-021, Spec FR-022, Spec FR-044]
- [x] CHK008 Does the main UX state taxonomy explicitly include unauthorized or token-required API states for non-loopback server access? [Resolved, Spec FR-036, Contract web-api-contract.md API Client Rules]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK009 Is the production tool-surface requirement clear enough to exclude landing-page or marketing composition on the first screen? [Clarity, Spec FR-001]
- [x] CHK010 Is the developer-tool shell requirement specific about repo switcher, selected repository status, global search, active navigation, and selected-context orientation? [Clarity, Spec FR-031]
- [x] CHK011 Are search result context requirements clear enough to let users choose the intended symbol without relying on implementation-specific UI text? [Clarity, Spec FR-006]
- [x] CHK012 Is the graph truncation requirement specific about explaining omitted data rather than merely limiting graph size? [Clarity, Spec FR-014, SC-004]
- [x] CHK013 Are mobile and narrow-layout requirements specific about responsive tabs or sheets, reachable controls, summary-first graph fallback, and avoiding required two-dimensional page scrolling outside the graph canvas? [Clarity, Spec FR-037]
- [x] CHK014 Are chat context-boundary requirements clear about visible graph-grounding limits and backend-owned context assembly? [Clarity, Spec FR-022, Spec FR-042]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK015 Do repo switcher, search, symbol detail, graph, impact, and chat requirements consistently preserve selected repository context where appropriate? [Consistency, Spec FR-031, Spec FR-032, SC-002]
- [x] CHK016 Do graph and symbol requirements consistently define selected-symbol context as synchronized between search, detail, canvas, and non-canvas summaries? [Consistency, Spec FR-007, Spec FR-013, Spec FR-033, Spec FR-035]
- [x] CHK017 Do chat requirements consistently keep browser requests limited to repo id, prompt, selected symbol, and view hints while the backend assembles graph context? [Consistency, Spec FR-020, Spec FR-042, Contract chat-adapter-contract.md]
- [x] CHK018 Do shadcn/Tailwind requirements align with the dense developer-tool layout decision rather than introducing decorative or marketing-style UI requirements? [Consistency, Spec FR-028, Plan Phase 0 Research Summary, Research R2]
- [x] CHK019 Do visual graph requirements and accessibility mirror requirements align without making canvas-only selection the sole access path? [Consistency, Spec FR-034, Spec FR-035]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK020 Are primary repo selection, freshness recognition, search, and symbol-opening outcomes measurable with a user time threshold? [Measurability, SC-001]
- [x] CHK021 Is movement from symbol details to graph exploration and back measurable without losing repository context? [Measurability, SC-002]
- [x] CHK022 Are graph render, interaction, nonblank canvas, and truncation outcomes measurable for representative and over-limit graph cases? [Measurability, SC-003, SC-004]
- [x] CHK023 Is impact review measurable by affected-file identification time when backend data is available? [Measurability, SC-005]
- [x] CHK024 Are desktop/mobile layout risks measurable through no critical text overlap, unreachable primary controls, or keyboard traps across required workflows? [Measurability, SC-009]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK025 Are primary navigation paths covered from repository overview to search, symbol detail, graph exploration, impact review, and chat context? [Coverage, Spec US1-US7, Spec FR-031, Spec FR-033]
- [x] CHK026 Are alternate and degraded scenarios covered for no repositories, stale or indexing repositories, no search results, ambiguous search results, unavailable relationships, graph truncation, and chat disabled or failed states? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases, Spec FR-036]
- [x] CHK027 Are recovery or continuation expectations documented so unaffected panels preserve shell and selected context while only the affected panel shows skeleton, empty, or error content? [Coverage, Clarifications UX and Navigation, Spec FR-036, Spec FR-037]
- [x] CHK028 Are offline or backend-unreachable scenarios covered separately from package no-CDN runtime validation? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases, Spec FR-023, SC-008]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK029 Are SPEC-005 API dependencies traceable for repository, status, search, node, relationship, graph, impact, flows, clusters, and re-analysis UX states? [Traceability, Spec FR-039, Spec FR-040, Contract web-api-contract.md]
- [x] CHK030 Are SPEC-018 chat dependencies traceable for configured, disabled, dormant, misconfigured, fallback, pending-bundle, rate-limited, unavailable, and error UX states? [Traceability, Spec FR-041, Spec FR-044, Contract chat-adapter-contract.md]
- [x] CHK031 Are renderer and shadcn style decisions traceable from planning research to UX requirements for graph interaction and dense developer-tool layout? [Traceability, Spec FR-030, Plan Phase 0 Research Summary, Research R1, Research R2]

## Notes

- Checklist focus: repo switcher, global search, symbol details, graph canvas, impact panel, chat panel, dense shadcn developer-tool layout, and graph/chat navigation continuity.
- Depth: Standard reviewer gate for Phase 4 UX requirements quality.
- Initial finding resolved: CHK008 is now covered by Spec FR-036 and RepositoryStatus state requirements.
- Rerun 2026-07-15: no additional UX requirement gaps found after remediation.
